prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
In the third quarter of this strange annum, I, Osbert the Noted, was retained by the Guild of Soft‑ware‑as‑Servitude to tally their tributes, and upon peering into their enchanted dash-borde (a luminous slate that hums) I beheld numerals mustered like spearmen upon a field.  
Their Annual Recurring Revenue did wax by three-and-twenty per hundred, wrought by “upsell” and “cross-sell,” which, if I grasp aright, is the art of persuading vassals to pay more silver for licenses that dwell entirely in a holy vapour they call the Cloud.  
A worm of misfortune named Churne gnawed at the village palisade, yet the knights of Customer Success, bearing smile-tokens and relics of N.P.S., drove it back so the Monthly Recurring Revenue cauldron yet simmereth.  
Meanwhile the burn rate burns like a votive before St. Cashflow, and the seers of Finance swear the coffers shall endure until next harvest if the engineers complete their sprinting around the Jira stone and the salesmen keep stuffing the Pipeline, which is both a pipe and, most vexingly, not a pipe.
